name: La Horizontal Trail
name_original: La Horizontal de Navafría — ida y vuelta por Parque Nacional de la Sierra de Guadarrama
description: Mountain bike the moderate La Horizontal Trail for 21.6 miles (34.7 km) through Valle del Lozoya in Sierra de Guadarrama National Park.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Aldealengua de Pedraza offers diverse terrain within the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park. The region features extensive pine forests, such as the Pinar de Navafría, and significant mountain peaks like Pico del Nevero. Riders can expect a mix of cultivated fields, open meadows, and elevated mountainous sections, providing varied riding experiences. The landscape is also characterized by numerous streams and offers panoramic views from higher elevations. Best mountain bike trails around Aldealengua De Pedraza The most popular mountain bike trail is La Horizontal Trail, a 21.6 miles (34.7 km) moderate route that takes about 3 hours 10 minutes to complete. This trail offers a balanced ride through varied landscapes. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Navafría Pass – Puerto de Linera loop from Gallegos, a difficult 31.9 miles (51.3 km) path. This route provides challenging ascents and rewarding views from Puerto de Linera. Local mountain bikers also love the Navafría Pass – Puerto de Navafría loop from Gallegos, a 33.3 miles (53.7 km) trail leading through high mountain terrain and pine forests, often completed in about 5 hours 31 minutes. Mountain biking around Aldealengua De Pedraza is defined by pine forests, challenging mountain peaks, and open meadows. The network offers options for both moderate and difficult ability levels. The routes in Aldealengua De Pedraza are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 500 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Aldealengua De Pedraza's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Aldealengua de Pedraza?
A: The trails in Aldealengua de Pedraza offer a diverse riding experience. You'll find routes traversing extensive pine forests, such as the Pinar de Navafría, as well as open meadows and cultivated fields. As the area is part of the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park, expect elevated, mountainous sections with varied surfaces, including some challenging ascents and descents.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of mountain bike trails around Aldealengua de Pedraza?
A: The mountain bike trails around Aldealengua de Pedraza cater to various skill levels. You'll find a good mix of routes, with 6 classified as &lt;strong&gt;moderate&lt;/strong&gt; and 8 as &lt;strong&gt;difficult&lt;/strong&gt;. This means there are options for riders looking for a balanced ride as well as those seeking more challenging ascents and technical sections.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: While there are no trails specifically rated as 'easy' in the immediate vicinity, the &lt;strong&gt;moderate&lt;/strong&gt; routes can be suitable for riders with some experience. For example, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1196857045/la-horizontal-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Horizontal Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 21.6-mile route that offers a balanced ride through varied landscapes, which might be a good starting point for improving riders or families with older children.

Q: What natural landmarks or viewpoints can I see while mountain biking?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty and offers several notable landmarks and viewpoints. You can encounter impressive peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4078211" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nevero Peak&lt;/a&gt;, which is the highest point of the Carpetanos Mountains and offers spectacular views. Other points of interest include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2347605" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Linera&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4003184" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navafría Pass&lt;/a&gt;. You might also come across the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3028592" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorrera de San Mamés&lt;/a&gt; waterfall or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4516598" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoyos de Pinilla&lt;/a&gt; lake.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes available?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes in the area are designed as loops, offering a complete riding experience. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20142535" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navafría Pass – Puerto de Linera loop from Gallegos&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20142572" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navafría Pass – Puerto de Navafría loop from Gallegos&lt;/a&gt;, both of which provide extensive rides through high mountain terrain and pine forests.

Q: How much elevation gain can I expect on mountain bike routes here?
A: The mountain bike routes around Aldealengua de Pedraza, especially those in the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park, often feature significant elevation gains. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20142572" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navafría Pass – Puerto de Navafría loop from Gallegos&lt;/a&gt; involves over 1,400 meters of ascent, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38150919" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arroyo del Roble – Puerto de Navafría loop from Gallegos&lt;/a&gt; has over 1,500 meters of climbing. Even moderate routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1196857045/la-horizontal-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Horizontal Trail&lt;/a&gt; include around 460 meters of elevation gain.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Aldealengua de Pedraza?
A: The region offers great mountain biking opportunities, but seasonal considerations are important. Spring and autumn generally provide the most pleasant temperatures and stable weather for riding. Summer can be hot, especially at lower elevations, while winter may bring snow and colder conditions to the higher mountain passes and peaks, making some routes more challenging or inaccessible.

Q: Do I need any special permits to mountain bike in the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park?
A: As a significant portion of Aldealengua de Pedraza lies within the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park, it's advisable to check the park's official regulations regarding cycling. While general access is often permitted on designated trails, specific rules might apply to certain areas or during particular seasons. Always respect park guidelines to preserve the natural environment.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about mountain biking in Aldealengua de Pedraza?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 60 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, from shaded pine forests to challenging mountain paths, and the stunning panoramic views from elevated points like Pico del Nevero and Puerto de Linera. The well-preserved natural environment within the National Park is also a frequently highlighted aspect.

Q: Are there any long-distance mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking longer distances and significant challenges, there are several options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38150919" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arroyo del Roble – Puerto de Navafría loop from Gallegos&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 36.3-mile route with over 1,500 meters of elevation gain, taking approximately 5 hours 48 minutes. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40008585" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nevero Peak – Navafría Pass loop from Gallegos&lt;/a&gt;, a 23.1-mile difficult route with over 1,100 meters of climbing.

Q: Is public transport available to reach the mountain bike trails?
A: Public transport options directly to the trailheads in Aldealengua de Pedraza are generally limited due to its rural location. It is often more convenient to reach the area by car, especially if you are bringing mountain bikes. Some routes might be accessible from nearby towns with bus connections, but direct access to specific trail starting points may require a short ride or taxi.

faqs.question: Is La Horizontal Trail suitable for beginner mountain bikers?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is considered quite accessible and suitable for beginners. It's primarily a doubletrack, meaning it's wide and generally less technical than singletrack trails. Its easy difficulty rating makes it a good option for those new to mountain biking or looking for a less challenging experience.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily follows a doubletrack, which is wide enough for two riders side-by-side. This indicates a smoother and less technical surface compared to singletrack trails, making it suitable for mountain biking, E-biking, and gravel biking.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead for La Horizontal Trail?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details are not provided, trails in the Valle del Lozoya region typically have designated parking areas near popular access points. It's advisable to check local maps or komoot's starting point for the most accurate trailhead parking information.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for mountain biking this route is approximately 3 hours and 10 minutes, covering a distance of about 34.7 kilometers. This duration can vary based on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: Are there any specific landmarks or points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes by several notable points. You'll encounter a Trail Junction in Sierra de Guadarrama National Park, as well as mountain passes like Puerto de Linera and Navafría Pass.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride La Horizontal Trail?
faqs.answer: While specific recommendations are not given, trails in the Valle del Lozoya region are generally best enjoyed during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can be hot, and winter may bring snow, especially at higher elevations within the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park.

faqs.question: Is La Horizontal Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As the trail is located within the Parque Nacional de la Sierra de Guadarrama, dogs are generally permitted but must be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Always follow national park regulations.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this trail or the Parque Nacional de la Sierra de Guadarrama?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access trails like La Horizontal within the Parque Nacional de la Sierra de Guadarrama for recreational use. However, always check the official park website for any updated regulations or specific activity permits that might be required.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route officially includes sections of "La Horizontal" (100%) and "La Horizontal de Navafría" (79%). It also briefly touches upon "[CIMA MA06] Navafría" (2%), "[CIMA SE03] Navafría" (2%), "La Horizontal de Navafría" (23%), "La Horizontal de Navafría" (2%), and "Pista esquí de fondo Navafría" (1%).

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: Located in the Valle del Lozoya within the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park, the trail offers natural beauty with views of the surrounding valley and potentially mountainous landscapes. The doubletrack nature allows for enjoying the scenery without overly technical riding.

faqs.question: Is La Horizontal Trail a loop or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: Based on the tour data, this specific komoot tour for La Horizontal Trail is described as "ida y vuelta" (out and back), meaning you will return along the same path you took to reach the turnaround point.
